content.problemStatement:
Chef has a list of non-overlapping intervals intervalListintervalListintervalList sorted by their start times. Each interval is represented as a pair of integers indicating the start and end points. 

Now, Chef wants to insert a new interval into this list newIntervalValuenewIntervalValuenewIntervalValue. After inserting, Chef needs to ensure that the list remains sorted by start times and that no intervals overlap. 

If any intervals overlap due to the insertion, Chef wants to merge them into a single interval. Help Chef by writing a function that returns the updated list of merged intervals.

Function DeclarationFunction NameinsertIntervalinsertIntervalinsertInterval - This function inserts a new interval into a list of non-overlapping intervals and merges any overlapping intervals.

Parameters
intervalListintervalListintervalList: A array of intervals where each interval is represented as a array of two integers [start,end][start, end][start,end]. The intervals are initially non-overlapping and sorted by start time.
newIntervalValuenewIntervalValuenewIntervalValue: A array of two integers representing the new interval to insert into intervalListintervalListintervalList.
Return Value
Returns a 2D array of integers representing the updated list of merged intervals.
Each interval is represented as a array of two integers [start,end][start, end][start,end].
constraints: The intervals in intervalList are sorted by their start times in ascending order.
content.inputFormat: First line contains T, the number of test cases. Each test case contains two lines: the first line contains a list of intervals where each interval is represented as a pair of integers [start, end], and the second line contains a new interval to insert into the list.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print the updated list of merged intervals after inserting the new interval. The intervals should be sorted by their start times and must not overlap.

sampleTestCases.explanation: The new interval [5, 10] overlaps with [6, 9], so we merge them into [5, 10]. The other intervals remain unchanged.
The new interval [3, 8] overlaps with [2, 4] and [7, 9], so we merge them into [2, 9]. The interval [12, 14] remains unchanged.
The new interval [17, 19] does not overlap with any existing intervals, so we simply append it to the end.

solution.approach: The approach is to iterate through the list of intervals and insert the new interval at the correct position. If the new interval overlaps with any existing intervals, merge them into a single interval. The time complexity is O(N) where N is the number of intervals, and the space complexity is O(N) for storing the merged intervals.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case, O(T * N) total.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N) — the dominant structure is the list of merged intervals.

code.Python3:
def insertInterval(intervals, newInterval):
    result = []
    i = 0
    n = len(intervals)

    # Add intervals that end before newInterval starts
    while i < n and intervals[i][1] < newInterval[0]:
        result.append(intervals[i])
        i += 1

    # Merge overlapping intervals
    while i < n and intervals[i][0] <= newInterval[1]:
        newInterval[0] = min(newInterval[0], intervals[i][0])
        newInterval[1] = max(newInterval[1], intervals[i][1])
        i += 1
    result.append(newInterval)

    # Add remaining intervals
    while i < n:
        result.append(intervals[i])
        i += 1

    return result
